commit.message:
Staging → Main (#159)

* chore: add staging branch workflow (#139)

Add staging as an intermediate branch between feature branches and main.
PRs now target staging for batch testing before a single production deploy.

- CI triggers on pushes/PRs to both main and staging
- E2E tests run on push to both main and staging
- CLAUDE.md updated with new branch workflow docs

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add promote-staging workflow with CI + deploy guards (#141)

Adds a manually triggered workflow that verifies CI and Vercel deployment
status on staging before merging to main. Prevents promoting broken builds.

Guards:
- CI must have passed on the latest staging commit
- Vercel deployment must have succeeded (or be absent)
- Uses --ff-only merge to keep history clean
- Auto-resets staging to main after promotion

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(e2e): use .first() for "Explore Events" link selectors

Homepage now has multiple "Explore Events" links (hero CTA + Strava
section), causing Playwright strict mode violations. Use .first() to
target the hero CTA.

Adds a workflow that runs when a PR merges into staging. It appends the
PR number and title to the staging → main PR description. Creates the
staging → main PR if one doesn't exist yet.

commit.message:
Perf : -65% tokens, -60% temps, cache correct partout

synthese.py
- Fix critique : cache ephemeral sur le template statique (~15k tokens)
  et non sur les documents (les retries beneficient maintenant du cache)
- max_tokens output : 16000 -> 10000 (suffisant pour une synthese)
- Regex compilees une seule fois au chargement du module (evite
  17 compilations a chaque appel de _extract_clinical_text)

ocr.py
- System prompt cache avec anthropic-beta prompt-caching (economie ~70%)
- Pages PDF fallback : ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) au lieu de
  boucle sequentielle -> PDFs 30 pages : 30min -> 8min
- JPEG qualite 85 au lieu de PNG : images 3x plus legeres -> moins de tokens

classifier.py
- System prompt + liste des types : precompiles au chargement du module
- Cache ephemeral sur le bloc statique (instructions + 11 types)
- Un seul champ user message contenant filename + sample

anonymizer.py
- detect_patient_name : system prompt cache (memes instructions pour
  tous les documents -> economie ~70% sur tokens instructions)
- process_ocr_results : detections en parallele via ThreadPoolExecutor(3)
  avant le groupage sequentiel (pas de race condition sur les dossiers)

config.py
- HAIKU_MAX_NEW_DOCS : 2 -> 5
- HAIKU_MAX_CHARS : 8_000 -> 30_000
  (Haiku utilise maintenant pour ~40% des syntheses incrementales)

commit.message:
fix: prefix should only be [summary, ack], not full compressor output

The prefix was including verbatim recent messages from the compressor,
but injection also appends messages[match_end:] from the original
request — causing duplicates (msg48-76 appearing twice). This broke
rolling compression because the key hashes had a repeated pattern
that could never match the original request.

Fix: prefix = compressed[:2] (just summary + ack). Verbatim messages
come from the original request during injection, no duplication.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Improve data structures, fix string truncation, and modernize Option predicate methods

IntervalTree: Added a free list (Vec<usize>) for node slot reuse.
When nodes are removed via remove_at, their indices are pushed onto
the free list instead of being permanently wasted. On insert, free
slots are reused before appending to the nodes Vec. This prevents
unbounded memory growth in long-running trees with frequent
insert/remove cycles. The remove helper now collects unlinked
indices and feeds them to the free list.

FenwickTree: Changed query_ops from u64 to AtomicU64 to allow
concurrent read-only queries (prefix_sum, range_sum, point_query)
without requiring &mut self. The Clone impl is now manual to handle
AtomicU64 correctly (loads with Relaxed ordering). Query methods
now atomically increment the counter via fetch_add.

Events (events.rs): Replaced the O(n) truncate_to_char_boundary
implementation (which iterated all char_indices up to max_len) with
a simple backward scan: start at max_len, decrement while not at a
char boundary. This is O(1) amortized since UTF-8 continuation bytes
are at most 3 bytes back from any position.

Subprocess bridge (subprocess_bridge.rs): Simplified timeout handling
and process lifecycle management. Reduced redundant match arms.

Modernized Option predicates across 10 modules: replaced
.map_or(true, |x| ...) with .is_none_or(|x| ...) and
.map_or(false, |x| ...) with .is_some_and(|x| ...) in
connector_event_model, connector_lifecycle, connector_mesh,
connector_registry, events, quantile_sketch, session_profiles,
swarm_scheduler, swarm_work_queue, and topology_orchestration.
These are Rust 1.82+ stabilized methods that express intent more
clearly.

Minor: runtime.rs and runtime_compat.rs formatting adjustments,
fleet_launcher.rs import cleanup, voi.rs simplified error handling,
wal_engine.rs minor refactor.

commit.message:
Refactor policy command rules, add redirect tracking to symbolic exec, and enrich MCP audit context

Policy engine (policy.rs): Removed the inline CommandRule table and
all associated static LazyLock regex patterns (RM_RF_ROOT, RM_RF_GENERIC,
GIT_RESET_HARD, GIT_CLEAN_FD, GIT_PUSH_FORCE, GIT_BRANCH_DELETE,
SQL_DESTRUCTIVE) along with the CommandGateDecision enum and CommandRule
struct. These patterns are now handled by the configurable policy rules
system that was introduced in the previous commit cycle, making the
hardcoded regex table redundant. The command_gate function's match-based
dispatch was simplified accordingly. Net: -171 lines of regex patterns
and boilerplate removed.

Approval system (approval.rs): The consume_with_context method now
records the actual actor kind (robot, mcp, workflow, human) in the
audit trail instead of always hardcoding "human". This was a parity
bug where robot-initiated approval consumptions were misattributed.
New test: consume_with_context_preserves_non_human_actor_kind_in_audit
verifies all actor kinds produce correct audit records.

MCP tools (mcp_tools.rs): Added structured decision context to audit
records for wa.events_annotate, wa.events_triage, wa.events_acknowledge,
wa.events_resolve, and wa.events_escalate tools. Each audit entry now
includes surface (mcp), actor_kind, tool name, action_kind, event_id,
operation, and actor_id. New helpers: serialize_mcp_audit_decision_context
and mcp_event_mutation_decision_context. Actor kind in audit records
changed from "robot" to "mcp" for MCP-initiated operations to
distinguish automation sources.

Symbolic execution (ars_symbolic_exec.rs): ParsedCommand now tracks
redirect operations via a new `redirects: Vec<(String, String)>` field
capturing (operator, target) pairs. The parse_commands function now
handles ShellToken::Redirect by looking ahead for the target word
token, supporting operators like >, >>, 2>, 2>&1, etc. The
build_command helper updated to accept and propagate redirect info.
This enables downstream policy rules to reason about output redirection
(e.g., detecting `> /etc/passwd` or `>> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys`).

ARS compiler (ars_compile.rs): Simplified matches_safety_regex by
removing the hand-rolled character class parser (char_in_class,
bracket pattern parsing) in favor of delegating to the regex crate.
The old implementation only handled simple `[charset]+` patterns and
silently allowed everything else; the new implementation correctly
validates all regex patterns.

ARS generalize (ars_generalize.rs): Simplified safety regex matching
to align with the compiler's approach.

ARS secret scan (ars_secret_scan.rs): Minor adjustments for the
simplified regex interface.

commit.message:
feat: HUD API rate protection with 429 exponential backoff (#59)

* feat: add HUD API rate protection with 429 exponential backoff

Fix coral-hud's failure caching which was completely ineffective:
readCacheFile returned cache.data (null for errors), collapsing
negative cache hits into cache misses and re-hitting the API on
every ~3s statusline invocation even during active 429 state.

- Cache contract: readCacheFile now returns full {ts,data,error,rateLimit}
  entry; callers distinguish error hits from misses explicitly
- normalizeCacheEntry/isFreshCacheEntry/readBackoffState/getCacheTtlMs
  helpers handle backward compat, non-finite timestamps, stale backoff
- Success TTL: 30s → 180s (ccstatusline parity)
- Error TTL: 15s → 30s
- 429 backoff: min(120s × 2^(n-1), 600s), resets after RATE_LIMIT_MAX_MS
- Both Claude and Codex APIs get identical cache/backoff policy
- Codex 401 refresh-retry path preserved; post-retry 429 enters backoff
- HUD script extracted from SKILL.md inline block to coral-hud.mjs
- SKILL.md install steps updated to read/compare sibling coral-hud.mjs

KB: statusline-negative-cache-contract, cache-nonfinite-timestamp-poisoning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace hardcoded Codex client_id and user_agent with dynamic values

client_id extracted from id_token JWT aud field in ~/.codex/auth.json.
user_agent version read from `codex --version` at runtime (~50ms).
Both were stale (0.104.0 vs actual 0.111.0) and would break on updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: remove Claude token refresh from HUD, match ccstatusline approach

Claude Code manages its own token lifecycle and writes back to
credentials.json. HUD doing its own refresh risked race conditions
and required a hardcoded OAUTH_CLIENT_ID. Now just reads the
current access token like ccstatusline does.

Removes: refreshToken(), OAUTH_CLIENT_ID, getCredentials() expiry check
Adds: getClaudeAccessToken() — simple credential read, no refresh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use full page navigation for cross-system auth redirects

When returnTo points to a different system (e.g. /idea-machina),
React Router's navigate() stays within RaamattuNytApp's router which
doesn't have that route, causing a 404. Now uses window.location.href
for cross-system URLs so detectActiveSystem() re-runs on full page load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show error state on Data Sources load failure instead of blank page

Add error/retry UI so a 503 from a bad server start doesn't silently
blank the page. Also fixes: always rebuild static assets in same commit.
